title: Diplacrum pygmaeum
intro: Diplacrum pygmaeum is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as a type of sedge. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Diplacrum pygmaeum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Diplacrum pygmaeum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in topical applications for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.

The plant is classified as a monocot, belonging to the larger group of monocots within the plant kingdom.
